title: Document360 Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Document360** is a SaaS knowledge-base platform exposing a versioned REST API under `https://apihub.document360.io/v2`. This connector — `Document360Connector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "document360"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Document360 project:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Projects | `/Projects` | List + read projects under the api_token |
| Versions | `/Projects/{projectId}/Versions` | List versions of a project |
| Languages | `/Projects/{projectId}/Languages` | List enabled languages |
| Categories | `/Categories/{versionId}` | List + create + update + delete category folders |
| Articles | `/Articles/{versionId}`, `/Articles/{articleId}` | CRUD + publish + read by language |
| Article versions | `/Articles/{articleId}/Versions` | Article revision history |
| Tags | `/Tags/{versionId}` | List tags scoped to a version |
| Drive | `/Drive/Files` | List + upload Drive (attachment) files |
| Team | `/TeamAccounts` | List team members and their roles |
| Templates | `/Templates/{versionId}` | List article templates |
| Search | `/Search` | Full-text search across articles |

The connector normalises articles into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::Document360HTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Document360 API token

1. Sign in to https://portal.document360.io.
2. Open the project you want to expose to Shielva.
3. Go to **Settings → Knowledge base portal → Extensions → API Tokens** (or the equivalent admin path for your plan).
4. Click **Generate API Token**. Pick the **scopes** you need:
   - `read:project` (mandatory for `health_check`)
   - `read:article` + `write:article` for sync + content CRUD
   - `read:category` + `write:category` to manage taxonomy
   - `read:drive` + `write:drive` for attachment endpoints
   - `read:team` to enumerate `TeamAccounts`
5. Copy the token value — Document360 shows it only once.
6. Note your **Project ID** and at least one **Version ID** from the Document360 dashboard URL bar (or from `GET /Projects` after install).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Document360**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_token` (secret) | Token from step 5 |
| `default_project_id` (text, optional) | Project to scope `sync()` to |
| `default_version_id` (text, optional) | Version to scope `sync()` to |
| `default_language_code` (text, optional) | ISO 639-1 code, default `en` |
| `project_slug` (text, optional) | Public site slug — used to synthesise canonical article URLs |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://apihub.document360.io/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_token` is present (does NOT call the API), initialises the `Document360HTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists projects as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 → `DEGRADED/UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 5xx → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` after retries are exhausted.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Document360 REST API uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_token` is sent in the **`api_token`** header. **Not** in `Authorization: Bearer <token>` — that is a Document360-specific gotcha that trips integrations migrating from other knowledge-base providers.
- No additional account/site headers are required (the token is bound to a Document360 account at generation time).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_token`. Initialises `Document360HTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Document360 endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(access_token=api_token, token_type="api_key")` and stores it via `set_token()`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /Projects` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | Local TokenInfo only; no Redis state required. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
api_token:    <api_token>           ← Document360 header name, NOT Authorization
Content-Type: application/json
Accept:       application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | `api_token` header value |
| `default_project_id` | text | no | `self.default_project_id` | Project sync() scopes to (else first visible project) |
| `default_version_id` | text | no | `self.default_version_id` | Version sync() scopes to (else walks all versions) |
| `default_language_code` | text | no (default `en`) | `self.default_language_code` | ISO 639-1 used by sync() and per-call default |
| `project_slug` | text | no | `self.project_slug` | Public site slug for canonical article URLs |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://apihub.document360.io/v2`) | Override for Document360 sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no (default 100) | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_BASE = "https://apihub.document360.io/v2"
_DEFAULT_LANG = "en"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `Document360Connector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::Document360HTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_token`; initialise `Document360HTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Wraps the static `api_token` in a `TokenInfo` and stores it via `set_token()`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Calls `GET /Projects`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Document360 articles → `NormalizedDocument` (id = `tenant_id_source_id`) → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Projects, Versions, Languages
children.content:
### `async list_projects() -> Any`
`GET /Projects` — list all projects accessible to the api_token.

### `async get_project(project_id) -> Any`
`GET /Projects/{project_id}`.

### `async list_versions(project_id) -> Any`
`GET /Projects/{project_id}/Versions`.

### `async list_languages(project_id) -> Any`
`GET /Projects/{project_id}/Languages` — list the languages enabled for the project.

children.title: Categories
children.content:
### `async list_categories(version_id, parent_category_id=None) -> Any`
`GET /Categories/{version_id}?parentCategoryId={parent}`.

### `async get_category(category_id) -> Any`
`GET /Categories/{category_id}`.

### `async create_category(version_id, parent_category_id, title, order=None, category_type="Folder", language_code="en") -> Any`
`POST /Categories/{version_id}` — body: `{title, parentCategoryId, categoryType, languageCode, order?}`.

### `async update_category(category_id, title=None, order=None) -> Any`
`PUT /Categories/{category_id}` — partial body.

### `async delete_category(category_id) -> Any`
`DELETE /Categories/{category_id}`.

children.title: Articles
children.content:
### `async list_articles(version_id, category_id=None, language_code="en") -> Any`
`GET /Articles/{version_id}?categoryId={cat}&languageCode={lang}`.

### `async get_article(article_id, language_code="en") -> NormalizedDocument`
`GET /Articles/{article_id}/Language/{language_code}` — returns a `NormalizedDocument` with id = `{tenant_id}_{article_id}` (tenant-prefixed for cross-tenant safety), HTML stripped to plain content, original HTML preserved in `metadata.raw_html`, and `source_url` synthesised from `project_slug`.

### `async create_article(version_id, category_id, title, content="", language_code="en", order=None) -> Any`
`POST /Articles/{version_id}` — body: `{title, content, categoryId, languageCode, order?}`.

### `async update_article(article_id, title=None, content=None, language_code="en") -> Any`
`PUT /Articles/{article_id}/Language/{language_code}` — partial body.

### `async delete_article(article_id) -> Any`
`DELETE /Articles/{article_id}`.

### `async publish_article(article_id, language_code="en") -> Any`
`POST /Articles/{article_id}/Language/{language_code}/Publish`.

### `async list_article_versions(article_id, language_code="en") -> Any`
`GET /Articles/{article_id}/Language/{language_code}/Versions` — revision history.

### `async search_articles(version_id, query, language_code="en", limit=20) -> Any`
`GET /Search?versionId=…&query=…&languageCode=…&limit=…`.

children.title: Tags · Team · Templates
children.content:
### `async list_tags(version_id) -> Any`
`GET /Tags/{version_id}`.

### `async list_team_members() -> Any`
`GET /TeamAccounts` — account-scoped, not version-scoped.

### `async list_templates(version_id) -> Any`
`GET /Templates/{version_id}`.

children.title: Drive (attachments)
children.content:
### `async list_drive_files(folder_id=None, page=1, page_size=50) -> Any`
`GET /Drive/Files?folderId=…&page=…&pageSize=…`.

### `async upload_drive_file(file_name, content_b64, folder_id=None) -> Any`
`POST /Drive/Files` — body: `{fileName, contentBase64, folderId?}`. Content MUST be base64-encoded; this lets the JSON body sail through standard HTTPS proxies without multipart edge cases.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
Document360Error                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── Document360AuthError                   # 401 / 403
├── Document360BadRequestError             # 400
├── Document360NotFound                    # 404 (alias: Document360NotFoundError)
├── Document360ConflictError               # 409
├── Document360RateLimitError              # 429 — carries retry_after_s
└── Document360NetworkError                # 5xx / network (alias: Document360ServerError)
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `min(base * 2 ** attempt + jitter, cap)`, retry up to `_DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.HTTPError` (timeout, network) | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `Document360NetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)   — after retries exhausted
```

All other public methods raise the typed exception — callers in `connector.py` catch the custom hierarchy only, never `httpx.HTTPError` directly (SOC).

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_token` blank | Re-enter in ACP. Document360 shows the token only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Document360 — token lacks scope | In Document360 → Settings → API Tokens, edit the token and grant `read:project` + the scopes for whichever surface you use (`read:article`, `read:drive`, `read:team`). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401 — token revoked or wrong account | Verify the token belongs to the account that owns the project. Rotate the token if needed. |
| `document360.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Document360 standard quota = 100/min) | The client retries with exponential backoff + jitter. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on a version-scoped call (Categories, Articles, Tags, Templates) | Wrong `version_id` for the project, or the version belongs to a different project | List versions via `list_versions(project_id)` and confirm the id. Versions are per-project — they do not cross-project. |
| 404 on `get_article` | Article exists but not in the requested language | Pass the correct `language_code` — Document360 stores per-language article bodies. Use `list_languages(project_id)` to enumerate. |
| 409 on `create_article` / `create_category` | Duplicate title or order conflict | Pick a unique title or omit `order` to let Document360 append. |
| `source_url` points to apidocs.document360.com | `project_slug` not set in install fields | Set `project_slug` in ACP — the connector will synthesise `https://<slug>.document360.io/<lang>/article/<id>` instead. |
| `Document360NetworkError: network error` after retries | Document360 outage or DNS / proxy issue | Check https://status.document360.com. The connector returns `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE)` so the gateway will pause syncs automatically. |
| Import error `cannot import name 'Document360NotFoundError'` | Older code on the legacy name | The alias `Document360NotFoundError = Document360NotFound` is preserved in `exceptions.py` — both import names work. |
